user.title: Smart Thermostat — Main entities
user.description: Connect the air conditioner and room sensors. These are the essential settings without which the thermostat cannot work.

data_description.name: The name this thermostat will appear as in Home Assistant. Example: 'Bedroom', 'Living room', 'Game room'.
data_description.climate_entity: The physical air conditioner this thermostat will control. Example: climate.ac_bedroom.
data_description.temp_sensor: Sensor measuring the real room temperature, placed away from the AC. Example: sensor.temperature_bedroom.
data_description.window_sensor: Binary sensor that turns 'on' when the window is open. Example: binary_sensor.window_bedroom.
data_description.presence_sensor: Optional presence sensor for fan boost. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.door_sensor: Optional door sensor. Sends open/close alerts, never acts on the AC. Leave empty to disable.

profilo.title: Regulation profile
profilo.description: Choose how the thermostat should behave. The profile determines how quickly the system cools and how sensitive it is to temperature changes.

data_description.profile:
🔵 BALANCED (recommended) — Turns on at +1.5°C above target, high fan from +2.0°C. Gradual cooling for everyday use.

🔴 AGGRESSIVE — Turns on at +1.2°C, high fan from +1.6°C. Cools faster, ideal for large or sun-exposed rooms.

🟢 GENTLE — Turns on at +1.8°C, high fan from +2.4°C. Quiet and discreet, perfect for sleeping.

⚙️ CUSTOM — Set every threshold manually in the next step.

soglie_termiche.title: Custom thermal thresholds
soglie_termiche.description: These parameters precisely control how the thermostat reacts to temperature. Only visible with the Custom profile.

data.below_offset: Margin below target to slow down (°C)
data.turn_on_offset: Minimum offset to turn on from solar (°C)
data.extreme_delta: Setpoint delta in extreme heat band (°C)

data_description.extreme_offset: Above target + this: max fan and very low setpoint. Example: 2.0°C → extreme heat above 27°C.
data_description.hot_offset: Above target + this: medium fan and low setpoint. Example: 1.5°C → strong heat between 26.5 and 27°C.
data_description.range_offset: Above target + this: low fan and slightly reduced setpoint. Example: 0.2°C → mild heat between 25.2 and 26.5°C.
data_description.below_offset: Below target - this: AC slows down to avoid over-cooling. Example: 0.1°C → slows below 24.9°C.
data_description.turn_on_offset: Turns on from solar only if room > target + this. Example: 1.5°C → only above 26.5°C.
data_description.temp_delta: How much the setpoint is lowered in normal bands. Example: 1.0°C → sends 24°C with target 25°C.
data_description.extreme_delta: How much the setpoint is lowered in extreme heat. Example: 2.0°C → sends 23°C in extreme heat.

energia.title: Solar — Automatic turn-on and turn-off
energia.description: Configure how the thermostat uses solar production to automatically control the AC. Leave sensors empty to completely disable this feature.

data.soc_min: Minimum battery charge to turn on (%)
data.fv_shutoff_delay_min: Consecutive insufficient samples before turning off
data.fv_shutoff_extra_hours: Extra hours after window end when it can still turn off

data_description.fv_sensor: Watts produced by panels in real time. Example: sensor.solar_panels_total.
data_description.fv_margin_w: Surplus required above consumption to turn on. Example: 1200W → turns on only if producing 1200W more than consuming.
data_description.soc_min: Minimum battery charge required to enable turn-on. Example: 70 → turns on only with battery ≥ 70%.
data_description.fv_start_time: Does not turn on automatically before this time. Example: 10:00.
data_description.fv_end_time: Does not turn on automatically after this time. Example: 16:00.
data_description.fv_priority: With multiple thermostats, lower number turns on first and off last. Example: bedroom=10, living=20.
data_description.fv_stagger_minutes: Pause between successive turn-ons to avoid consumption peaks. Example: 5 minutes.
data_description.fv_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: turns off the AC when solar surplus stays below threshold for N consecutive samples. With multiple thermostats, turns off the highest priority number first.
data_description.fv_shutoff_delay_min: Number of consecutive samples below threshold before turning off. The window is sliding: one high value does not reset the buffer, it just shifts it. Example: 5 samples at 1-min interval = 5 confirmed minutes.
data_description.fv_shutoff_threshold: The surplus (production - consumption) must be below this value in ALL the last N samples to trigger turn-off. Value 0 = turns off when solar no longer covers consumption. Positive value = turns off earlier, when surplus drops below X Watts. Example: 500W → turns off when surplus is below 500W for N consecutive samples.
data_description.fv_shutoff_extra_hours: Extra hours after window end when it can still turn off. Example: window ends at 16:00, extra 1h → can turn off until 17:00.

modalita_notturna.title: Night mode
modalita_notturna.description: Configure the thermostat's night-time behaviour. You can raise the target slightly, enable automatic turn-on, set automatic turn-off when target is reached, and turn off the AC at the end of night mode.

data.night_offset: Extra degrees added to target at night (°C)
data.night_turn_on_offset: Degrees above night target to turn on (°C)
data.night_shutoff_enabled: ▶ Automatic turn-off when target is reached
data.night_shutoff_min: Consecutive minutes at temperature before turning off
data.night_end_shutoff_enabled: ▶ Turn off AC at the end of night mode (always)
data.night_end_shutoff_auto_only: ▶ Turn off at end of night only if turned on automatically
data.presence_boost_enabled: ▶ Fan boost if presence detected and still warm
data.presence_boost_minutes: Minutes of continuous presence before boost
data.presence_boost_offset: Degrees above target to trigger boost (°C)
data.window_delay_minutes: Minutes with window open before turning off AC
data.min_below_internal: Minimum setpoint margin below internal sensor (°C)

data_description.night_start_time: Night mode starts at this time. The window can span midnight. Example: 23:00.
data_description.night_end_time: Night mode ends at this time. Example: 07:00.
data_description.night_offset: At night the effective target becomes target + this value. Example: target 25°C + 1°C = night target 26°C. Set 0 to keep the same target.
data_description.night_ac_enabled: If enabled: turns on the AC at night if the room exceeds night target + threshold, without needing solar.
data_description.night_turn_on_offset: How much the room must exceed the night target to trigger automatic turn-on. Example: night target 26°C + 1.5°C → turns on above 27.5°C.
data_description.night_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: turns off the AC when the room stays at temperature for the configured minutes. Works regardless of how it was turned on.
data_description.night_shutoff_delta: Tolerance above night target. Example: target 26°C, delta 0.3°C → 'at temperature' when ≤ 26.3°C. If it rises above, countdown restarts.
data_description.night_shutoff_min: Consecutive minutes at temperature before turning off. Example: 30 minutes.
data_description.night_end_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: when night mode ends (e.g. at 07:00), the AC is turned off ALWAYS, regardless of why it was on — manual, solar, or automatic night turn-on. Useful to avoid leaving the AC running all morning.
data_description.night_end_shutoff_auto_only: If enabled: when night mode ends, the AC is turned off ONLY IF it was turned on automatically by night mode. If you turned it on manually or via solar, nothing happens. Can be enabled together with 'always' — in that case 'always' takes precedence.
data_description.presence_boost_enabled: If enabled: raises fan to medium when presence is detected continuously and the room is still warm.
data_description.presence_boost_minutes: Minutes of continuous presence before boost activates. Example: 10 minutes.
data_description.presence_boost_offset: Boost only if room > target + this. Example: 1°C → boost only above 26°C with target 25°C.
data_description.window_delay_minutes: Minutes to wait before turning off due to open window. Example: 5 minutes → alerts immediately, turns off after 5 minutes.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the thermostat checks and updates. Example: 5 minutes.
data_description.calibration_max_offset: Maximum correction for difference between room sensor and AC internal sensor. Example: 3°C.
data_description.min_below_internal: The setpoint always stays at least this value below the AC internal sensor. Example: 1°C → if AC reads 24°C internally, setpoint never exceeds 23°C.

notifiche.title: Notifications and quiet hours
notifiche.description: Configure which devices the thermostat speaks and writes to. You can separately choose what to receive on Google Home and Telegram, and during which hours to silence alerts.

data.tts_media_players: Google Home / media player devices for voice
data.notify_chat_ids: Telegram chat IDs (alternative to notify entities)
data.notify_temp_change_enabled: ▶ Telegram notification on every setpoint change
data.notify_temp_change_limit_min: Minimum interval between setpoint change notifications (min)
data.notify_power_tts: ▶ Google voice for automatic solar/night turn-on/off
data.notify_night_end_tts: ▶ Google voice when AC turns off at end of night mode
data.notify_night_end_notify: ▶ Telegram when AC turns off at end of night mode
data.door_alert_enabled: ▶ Alert for door opening and closing
data.quiet_tts: ▶ Silence Google voice during quiet hours

data_description.tts_media_players: Google Home speakers or media_player entities for voice messages. Example: media_player.google_bedroom.
data_description.tts_engine_entity: TTS engine to use. Leave empty to use the first available in Home Assistant.
data_description.tts_message_open: Message spoken when window opens. Variables: {{ delay }}, {{ name }}, {{ target }}.
data_description.notify_targets: HA notify entities for Telegram. Example: notify.telegram_user.
data_description.notify_chat_ids: Telegram chat IDs separated by comma. Only if you have no notify entities.
data_description.notify_temp_change_enabled: If enabled: Telegram on every setpoint change. Can be frequent.
data_description.notify_temp_change_limit_enabled: If enabled: setpoint change Telegram notifications are sent at most once every X minutes. Useful to reduce messages when the thermostat frequently adjusts the setpoint. Quiet hours always take precedence and block notifications regardless of this setting.
data_description.notify_temp_change_limit_min: How many minutes must pass between setpoint change notifications. Example: 30 minutes → even if the setpoint changes every 5 minutes, a notification arrives at most every 30 minutes.
data_description.notify_temp_change_message: Variables: {{ name }}, {{ old_temp }}, {{ new_temp }}, {{ fan_mode }}, {{ room_temp }}, {{ target }}.
data_description.notify_power_tts: If enabled: Google Home announces when the AC turns on/off automatically due to solar or night mode, explaining the reason.
data_description.notify_power_notify: If enabled: Telegram when AC turns on/off automatically due to solar or night mode. Enabled by default.
data_description.notify_night_end_tts: If enabled: Google Home announces when the AC is turned off at the end of night mode. Separate channel from other alerts — you can keep this on even if others are silenced.
data_description.notify_night_end_notify: If enabled: Telegram receives a message when the AC turns off at the end of night mode. Separate channel. Enabled by default so you always know when the AC switches off in the morning.
data_description.door_alert_enabled: If enabled: alerts on both door opening and closing. Informational only, never acts on the AC.
data_description.door_alert_tts: Door alert via Google Home. Requires 'Door alert' enabled.
data_description.door_alert_notify: Door alert via Telegram. Requires 'Door alert' enabled.
data_description.quiet_enabled: If enabled: during the configured time window, some alerts are silenced according to preferences below.
data_description.quiet_start_time: Quiet period starts at this time. Can span midnight. Example: 23:00.
data_description.quiet_end_time: Quiet period ends at this time. Example: 07:00.
data_description.quiet_tts: If enabled: Google Home does not speak during quiet hours for automatic turn-on/off alerts.
data_description.quiet_notify: If enabled: Telegram suspends automatic turn-on/off messages during quiet hours.

abort.already_configured: This air conditioner is already managed by another Smart Thermostat FV instance.

sensori.title: Entities and sensors
sensori.description: Update the controlled air conditioner and connected sensors.

data_description.name: The name this thermostat appears as in Home Assistant. Example: 'Bedroom'.
data_description.temp_sensor: Room temperature probe, away from the AC.
data_description.window_sensor: Binary sensor 'on' when the window is open.
data_description.presence_sensor: Optional. Enables fan boost for continuous presence. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.door_sensor: Optional. Alerts when door opens or closes. Never acts on the AC. Leave empty to disable.

profilo.title: Regulation profile
profilo.description: Choose how the thermostat should behave.

data_description.profile:
🔵 BALANCED — Turns on at +1.5°C, high fan from +2.0°C.
🔴 AGGRESSIVE — Turns on at +1.2°C, high fan from +1.6°C. For large or sun-exposed rooms.
🟢 GENTLE — Turns on at +1.8°C, high fan from +2.4°C. Quiet, ideal for sleeping.
⚙️ CUSTOM — Set every threshold manually in the next step.

data.below_offset: Margin below target to slow down (°C)
data.turn_on_offset: Minimum offset to turn on from solar (°C)

data_description.extreme_offset: Above target + this: max fan. Example: 2.0°C → extreme heat above 27°C.
data_description.hot_offset: Above target + this: medium fan. Example: 1.5°C → strong heat between 26.5 and 27°C.
data_description.range_offset: Above target + this: low fan. Example: 0.2°C → mild heat between 25.2 and 26.5°C.
data_description.below_offset: Below target - this: AC slows down. Example: 0.1°C → slows below 24.9°C.
data_description.turn_on_offset: Turns on from solar only if room > target + this. Example: 1.5°C → only above 26.5°C.
data_description.temp_delta: How much the setpoint is lowered in normal bands. Example: 1.0°C.
data_description.extreme_delta: How much the setpoint is lowered in extreme heat. Example: 2.0°C.

energia.title: Solar — Automatic turn-on and turn-off
energia.description: Configure solar usage. Leave sensors empty to disable.

data.soc_min: Minimum battery charge to turn on (%)
data.fv_shutoff_delay_min: Consecutive insufficient samples before turning off
data.fv_shutoff_extra_hours: Extra hours after window end for turn-off

data_description.fv_sensor: Watts produced by panels in real time.
data_description.fv_margin_w: Surplus required above consumption to turn on. Example: 1200W.
data_description.soc_min: Minimum battery charge to enable turn-on. Example: 70%.
data_description.fv_start_time: Does not turn on automatically before this time.
data_description.fv_end_time: Does not turn on automatically after this time.
data_description.fv_priority: Lower number = turns on first and off last.
data_description.fv_stagger_minutes: Pause between successive turn-ons to avoid peaks.
data_description.fv_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: turns off when surplus stays below threshold for N consecutive samples.
data_description.fv_shutoff_delay_min: Number of consecutive samples below threshold before turning off. Sliding window: one high value shifts the buffer rather than resetting it.
data_description.fv_shutoff_threshold: Minimum surplus (production - consumption) required. Below this for N samples → turns off. Value 0 = turns off when solar no longer covers consumption.
data_description.fv_shutoff_extra_hours: Extra hours after window end when it can still turn off.

data.night_offset: Extra degrees added to target at night (°C)
data.night_turn_on_offset: Degrees above night target to turn on (°C)
data.night_shutoff_enabled: ▶ Automatic turn-off when target is reached
data.night_shutoff_min: Consecutive minutes at temperature before turning off
data.night_end_shutoff_enabled: ▶ Turn off AC at the end of night mode (always)
data.night_end_shutoff_auto_only: ▶ Turn off at end of night only if turned on automatically
data.presence_boost_enabled: ▶ Fan boost if presence detected and still warm
data.presence_boost_minutes: Minutes of continuous presence before boost
data.window_delay_minutes: Minutes with window open before turning off
data.min_below_internal: Minimum setpoint margin below internal sensor (°C)

data_description.night_start_time: Night mode starts. Can span midnight. Example: 23:00.
data_description.night_offset: Degrees added to target at night. Example: target 25°C + 1°C = night target 26°C.
data_description.night_ac_enabled: If enabled: turns on at night if room exceeds night target + threshold.
data_description.night_turn_on_offset: Threshold above night target to trigger turn-on. Example: night target 26°C + 1.5°C → turns on above 27.5°C.
data_description.night_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: turns off when room stays at temperature for configured minutes.
data_description.night_shutoff_delta: Tolerance above night target. Example: target 26°C, delta 0.3°C → at temperature when ≤ 26.3°C.
data_description.night_shutoff_min: Consecutive minutes at temperature before turning off. If temp rises, countdown restarts.
data_description.night_end_shutoff_enabled: If enabled: at the end of night mode the AC is turned off ALWAYS, regardless of why it was on.
data_description.night_end_shutoff_auto_only: If enabled: at the end of night mode turns off ONLY IF the AC was turned on automatically by night mode. If you turned it on manually, nothing happens.
data_description.presence_boost_enabled: If enabled: raises fan to medium when presence is continuous and room is still warm.
data_description.presence_boost_minutes: Minutes of continuous presence before boost. Example: 10 minutes.
data_description.presence_boost_offset: Boost only if room > target + this. Example: 1°C → boost only above 26°C.
data_description.window_delay_minutes: Minutes before turning off for open window. Example: 5 minutes.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the thermostat checks. Example: 5 minutes.
data_description.calibration_max_offset: Maximum correction for room vs internal sensor difference. Example: 3°C.
data_description.min_below_internal: Setpoint stays at least this value below the AC internal sensor. Example: 1°C.

notifiche.title: Notifications and quiet hours
notifiche.description: Configure voice and Telegram alerts, and the hours during which automatic alerts are silenced.

data.notify_temp_change_enabled: ▶ Telegram notification on every setpoint change
data.notify_temp_change_limit_min: Minimum interval between setpoint change notifications (min)
data.notify_power_tts: ▶ Google voice for automatic solar/night turn-on/off
data.notify_night_end_tts: ▶ Google voice when AC turns off at end of night mode
data.notify_night_end_notify: ▶ Telegram when AC turns off at end of night mode
data.door_alert_enabled: ▶ Alert for door opening and closing
data.quiet_tts: ▶ Silence Google voice during quiet hours

data_description.tts_media_players: Google Home speakers or media_player entities for voice messages.
data_description.tts_engine_entity: TTS engine. Leave empty for the default.
data_description.tts_message_open: Message when window opens. Variables: {{ delay }}, {{ name }}, {{ target }}.
data_description.notify_targets: HA notify entities for Telegram. Example: notify.telegram_user.
data_description.notify_chat_ids: Telegram chat IDs separated by comma. Only if you have no notify entities.
data_description.notify_temp_change_enabled: If enabled: Telegram on every setpoint change. Can be frequent.
data_description.notify_temp_change_limit_enabled: If enabled: setpoint change Telegram notifications are sent at most once every X minutes. Useful to reduce messages when the thermostat frequently adjusts the setpoint. Quiet hours always take precedence and block notifications regardless of this setting.
data_description.notify_temp_change_limit_min: How many minutes must pass between setpoint change notifications. Example: 30 minutes → even if the setpoint changes every 5 minutes, a notification arrives at most every 30 minutes.
data_description.notify_temp_change_message: Variables: {{ name }}, {{ old_temp }}, {{ new_temp }}, {{ fan_mode }}, {{ room_temp }}, {{ target }}.
data_description.notify_power_tts: If enabled: Google Home announces automatic turn-on/off due to solar or night mode.
data_description.notify_power_notify: If enabled: Telegram for automatic turn-on/off due to solar or night mode.
data_description.notify_night_end_tts: If enabled: Google Home announces when AC turns off at end of night mode. Separate channel.
data_description.notify_night_end_notify: If enabled: Telegram when AC turns off at end of night mode. Separate channel. Enabled by default.
data_description.door_alert_enabled: If enabled: alerts on both opening and closing. Informational only.
data_description.quiet_enabled: If enabled: during the configured window, some alerts are silenced.
data_description.quiet_start_time: Quiet period starts. Can span midnight.
data_description.quiet_tts: If enabled: Google Home silent during quiet hours for automatic alerts.
data_description.quiet_notify: If enabled: Telegram suspends automatic messages during quiet hours.
